key_departures.impact: Released with post-June 1 designation. Historic $99.2M dead cap hit split across two years: $67.4M in 2026 and $31.8M in 2027. Was benched twice late in 2025 season. Dolphins absorbing the largest dead cap hit in NFL history to move on.

Released in February 2026 to clear cap space. Eight-time Pro Bowler, five-time All-Pro was the offense's primary weapon. Was rehabbing a major leg injury from 2025. Release cleared substantial cap space but blew up the WR room.

Traded to Jets for a 2026 seventh-round pick (originally from Chargers). Was due $15.6M in 2026 on the final year of his deal. Trade freed approximately $5.9M in cap space. Dolphiins' starting safety is now gone, creating a hole at the position.

key_departures.new_team: Carolina Panthers (via Eagles trade at 2025 deadline)
key_departures.impact: Was traded to Eagles at the November 2025 deadline for a third-round pick. Phillips then departed Philadelphia in free agency and signed a 4-year, $120M deal with Carolina. Miami received a 3rd-round pick in exchange but lost their top EDGE rusher. Chop Robinson is the only experienced EDGE remaining on roster.

Released (expected as post-June 1 designation) to clear approximately $20M in cap savings. Was already part of a stripped-down EDGE group. Departure further compounds the pass-rush problem alongside the Phillips departure.
Released February 2026 as part of cap-clearing purge. Was the team's starting guard before suffering an injury in 2025. Interior OL was already a need; this departure deepens it.

Departed to the Chargers (following former HC Mike McDaniel) on a 2-year, $13M deal. Was the starting right guard for most of 2025. Graded as below-average by PFF (58th of 81 guards), but still a depth loss at a thin position.

Released then signed with Chargers. Was a 2023 Pro Bowler and scheme-critical fullback under Mike McDaniel. New HC Jeff Hafley comes from Green Bay, which does not roster a traditional fullback, so positional need is minimal.
Released February 2026 as part of cap purge. Was a depth receiver; departure is a minor roster hole given the WR room was already thin post-Hill.
Released after 8 seasons. Missed entire 2025 season on IR. Dolphins replaced him with Zane Gonzalez. Positional need immediately addressed.
Unrestricted free agent departing. Was a starting corner. Compounds an already thin secondary being further depleted with Fitzpatrick's trade.
Was the team's No. 1 cornerback entering 2025 before missing the entire season with a torn ACL. UFA departure removes a key CB from a secondary that is being rebuilt from scratch.
Starter-caliber corner departing as UFA. Combined with Douglas and Kohou departures, the CB room has been essentially gutted.

signings.analysis: Three-year deal with $45M fully guaranteed across the first two years, including a $22.25M signing bonus. Willis was electric in relief appearances for Green Bay over two seasons (6 starts total, 972 passing yards, 6 TD, 0 INT, plus 161 rushing yards and 3 TD). Deal is structured cap-friendly in 2026 (~$8.6M hit) with a ramp-up in 2027. AAV of $22.5M is solidly in the mid-starter tier — not elite money, but a real commitment. Two years of effective guarantees make this a legitimate bridge-to-starter contract. QB need is fully addressed for 2026; the team may still want a developmental QB in the draft given the 3-year window, but the starter job is settled.

One-year prove-it deal to retain Dulcich, who was a bright spot in 2025 (26 rec, 336 yards, 1 TD in just 10 games) leading all TEs in YAC (7.6) and yards per target (10.2). The one-year structure signals this is a bridge, not a long-term solution at TE. At $4M AAV, this is below starter-tier money at the position. TE remains a genuine need for the long term — Miami lacks a true starter-caliber TE on a multi-year deal. The draft should still be a target for TE.

One-year deal replacing the released Jason Sanders. Gonzalez was 19-of-22 on field goals in 2025 (86.4%) and 7-of-9 from 50+. Reliable inside 40 yards and improving at distance. Kicker is addressed for 2026. One-year nature means K could resurface as a need in 2027, but for draft purposes K is off the board.

Veteran minimum re-sign of a 7th-round 2022 draft pick who is primarily a special-teamer (15 ST stops in 2025, just 7 defensive tackles). Has 29 career tackles, 2 QB hits, and 1 PBU in 37 games — zero sacks. This is roster retention at the minimum, not an EDGE need solution. EDGE remains one of Miami's most critical draft needs.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Willis signing (3yr/$67.5M, $45M guaranteed) fully addresses the starter need for 2026-2027. The cap-friendly structure and dual-threat ability make him a legitimate investment. Team may still want a developmental QB in rounds 4-7 to build depth behind Willis, but QB is no longer a premium draft need.

This is now Miami's single most critical draft need. Both Jaelan Phillips (traded away) and Bradley Chubb (released) are gone. Only Chop Robinson has notable NFL experience at the position. Cameron Goode's re-sign at the veteran minimum is a special-teamer, not a starter solution. EDGE must be a top priority in the 2026 Draft — likely Round 1 at pick 11.

Tyreek Hill's release left a massive void at WR1. No meaningful WR additions were made in free agency. Dulcich is a TE, not a WR solution. The roster's top receivers are unproven young players. WR is now a co-equal Tier 1 need alongside EDGE — elite or near-elite WR talent must be targeted in the draft.

The secondary has been gutted. Jack Jones, Rasul Douglas, and Kader Kohou (coming off torn ACL) are all UFAs who appear to be departing. Minkah Fitzpatrick was traded. Only young developmental DBs remain. Miami must address CB in the draft, either at 11th overall or aggressively in Days 2-3.

Fitzpatrick's trade to the Jets removes the only Pro Bowl-caliber safety on the roster. No safety was added in free agency. Ashtyn Davis and Ifeatu Melifonwu are UFA-status depth pieces. Safety is a genuine Tier 1 need — either in the draft or via a late-free-agency addition.

James Daniels (released) and Cole Strange (Chargers) both departed. The interior OL has two starters gone with no replacements signed. Aaron Brewer at center is the lone confirmed starter. Guards are a significant void. IOL must be addressed in the draft and potentially via remaining free agents.

Dulcich re-signed on a 1-year deal — a bridge, not a solution. He's produced in limited snaps but has no multi-year commitment. Miami should target a TE in rounds 2-4 of the draft to build long-term at the position.

key_additions.headline: New franchise QB replaces Tua — dual-threat passer with 2yr of effective guarantees becomes immediate Day 1 starter
Miami retains breakout TE on 1-year deal after he led all TEs in YAC and yards per target in 2025
Reliable veteran kicker (19/22 FG in 2025) fills void left by released Jason Sanders
Special-teams ace retained at veteran minimum — depth only, does not address starter EDGE need

key_losses.headline: Franchise QB released in historic cap-clearing move — largest dead cap hit in NFL history ends the Tua era in Miami
8x Pro Bowl WR1 released while rehabbing major leg injury — leaves massive void as Miami's offense is rebuilt from scratch
Pro Bowl safety traded to division rival Jets for a late pick — secondary now a top-priority rebuild zone

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded to Eagles (2025 deadline) for 2026 3rd-round pick; signed 4yr/$120M with Panthers
key_losses.headline: First-round EDGE rusher traded mid-season; now signed a $120M Panthers deal — Dolphins got a 3rd-round pick and validation of the move
Former 1st-round EDGE released to clear cap — Miami now has no established starter at the position
Starting guard released in offseason purge — interior OL now has two starter vacancies heading into the draft
Starting RG follows ex-HC Mike McDaniel to LAC — compounds Miami's interior OL crisis

notes: Christian Harris (LB) appears on some trackers under MIA but is confirmed signed with the Atlanta Falcons per multiple sources (Cameron Wolfe/NFL Media). He is NOT a Miami signing and has been excluded. Bradley Chubb's post-June 1 release had not become official as of March 11 but is confirmed by team intent — cap impact will hit in June. Fitzpatrick trade receives only a 7th-round pick in return; deal reflects his expiring contract status more than his on-field value (81.8 PFF grade in 2025). Cap space figures are highly fluid due to the post-June 1 Tua dead cap ($67.4M) not yet hitting the books. OverTheCap listed -$1.78M in current cap space as of March 11 before the Tua hit processes.
